item.title: Retail Investors Are Showing Signs of Fatigue as Stocks Falter
item.description: The most reliable US stock buyers turned into sellers this week.

item.title: Iran Demands Crew, Cargo Details to Send Ships Through Hormuz
item.description: Vessels seeking to transit the Strait of Hormuz under Iranian protection are being asked to provide lists of crew and cargo, along with voyage details and bills of lading, in order to secure a green light from the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Oil Theft Is Burning a Billion-Dollar Hole in West Texas’ Economy
item.description: When it was $65 a barrel, crude was already going missing from Permian oil fields at record rates. Now that it’s almost $100, authorities are bracing for more pain.

item.title: Riksbank Minutes Show Commitment to Steady Rate Despite Iran War
item.description: Officials at the Riksbank believe steady Swedish borrowing costs are keeping inflation on track to their 2% target even with the risk of higher price growth triggered by the Iran war, the minutes of the latest policy meeting showed.

item.title: Ex-Houlihan Tech Bankers Launch Digital Infrastructure Fund
item.description: Two London-based technology bankers at Houlihan Lokey Inc. have left the advisory firm to launch a fund investing in the booming market for digital infrastructure.

item.title: Lanxess Soars as JPMorgan’s Double Upgrade Provides Rare Boost
item.description: Lanxess AG shares soared after JPMorgan Chase & Co. double upgraded the German specialty chemical company, saying it should get an earnings boost as the war in Iran eases competitive pressures and increases pricing power.

item.title: Hungary Touts Yuan Swap Line as Orban Seeks Chinese Financing
item.description: The Hungarian central bank’s swap line with the People’s Bank of China provides a source of stability to the nation’s funding amid market volatility, according to a senior official.

item.title: Fosun Resort Chain Club Med Is Said to Consider Hong Kong for IPO
item.description: Club Med SAS is considering an initial public offering and is looking at Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Turkey Is Urging Gulf Arab States Not to Join War Against Iran
item.description: Turkey is conducting intensive diplomatic efforts to try and prevent Gulf Arab countries from joining the US-Israeli war against Iran, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Signs of Jet Fuel Hoarding Emerge in Asia on Iran Oil Shock
item.description: Signs are growing that Asian countries are hoarding jet fuel after the Iran war sent oil prices surging, reflecting growing strain on the aviation industry.

item.title: HSBC Sees ‘Explosion’ in Estate Planning Demand as Risks Rise
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc’s insurance chief said the company is seeing an “explosion” in demand for estate planning and wealth transfer solutions as clients seek to de-risk from global uncertainty.

item.title: Kuaishou Tech Sales Climb as AI Monetization Gains Momentum
item.description: Kuaishou Technology said the rapid scaling up of its generative artificial intelligence tools drove revenue growth.

item.title: German Business Outlook Sinks as Iran Puts Recovery ‘on Ice’
item.description: Germany’s business outlook soured as higher energy prices due to the Iran war threaten to derail its nascent economic revival.

item.title: Oil Producers Getting Rapid Permits to Revive Old Nigeria Wells
item.description: Nigeria has slashed the time it takes to approve applications to revive idle oil wells from weeks to hours as Africa’s top crude producer seeks to take advantage of high energy prices.

item.title: Spacecraft Firm Pave Raises $40 Million Amid European Space Push
item.description: Swiss startup Pave Space closed a $40 million seed financing round to build hardware that can move satellites or cargo to different orbits, as Europe attempts to reduce its reliance on the US to access space.

item.title: The Death Spiral of a $250 Million Satellite Startup
item.description: Theia raised big money to launch a next-level network of digital imagery, but its legacy is mostly lawsuits, including federal fraud charges.

item.title: Autonomous Driving Firm DeepRoute.ai Is Said to Consider Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Chinese autonomous driving company DeepRoute.ai is considering an initial public offering in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, joining a growing list of tech firms selling shares in the Asian hub.

item.title: Iran’s Harder-to-Hunt Long-Range Missiles Taking Greater Toll
item.description: More than three weeks of unrelenting US and Israeli airstrikes have severely weakened Tehran’s military, but what remains of its vast missile arsenal is being used more efficiently from hard-to-reach bases in eastern Iran.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees More Gains Ahead for Europe’s Energy Stocks
item.description: Europe’s energy sector has room to extend its outperformance versus the broader market as investors are only beginning to price in a structural shift in supply risks amid war in the Middle East, according to analysts at Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Bezos Earth Fund to Back Gabon’s $200 Million Fund for Nature
item.description: Gabon is mobilizing $200 million from the likes of Bezos Earth Fund to help protect at least 30% of its terrestrial, fresh-water and marine ecosystems by 2030.

item.title: Asos Shares Jump as Womenswear Boosts Retailer’s Profitability
item.description: Asos Plc’s shares rose the most in four months after the online fast-fashion chain reported a 50% increase in underlying profits, driven by customer growth and strong performance in womenswear.

item.title: ECB Won’t Be ‘Paralyzed by Hesitation’ on Iran, Lagarde Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will act decisively and swiftly if the current surge in energy costs risks a broader bout of inflation, though for now it’s still assessing the shock caused by the Iran war, according to President Christine Lagarde.

item.title: Philippines Says It Can Intervene to Ensure Airlines Secure Fuel
item.description: The Philippine government can back efforts of airlines to secure jet fuel, reducing the risk of aircraft groundings as the global oil shortage triggered by the Iran war threatens air travel, according to the nation’s transportation chief.

item.title: Oreo-Maker Mondelez Joins Rush to Tap Swiss Franc Bond Market
item.description: Mondelez International, Inc. is tapping the Swiss franc bond market for the first time, joining a flurry of international firms turning to the currency for debt.

item.title: Iran War Shows Limits of BRICS as India Pushed to Choose Sides
item.description: India is facing mounting pressure as leader of the BRICS group to steer the bloc toward taking a firmer stand on the Iran conflict, putting New Delhi’s diplomacy to the test.

item.title: Nickel Spikes After Indonesia’s President Approves Export Tax
item.description: Nickel prices jumped after the world’s largest producer Indonesia agreed to tax outbound shipments of the battery metal.

item.title: India Refiners Tap Dollar Alternatives to Buy Russian Oil
item.description: Indian refiners are increasingly settling purchases of Russian oil in alternative currencies, according to people familiar with the matter, as they seek to reduce reliance on the dollar amid rising geopolitical tensions and shifts in US policy.

item.title: The Petrochemicals Shock That's Already Rippling Through Plastics
item.description: Another supply chain disruption.

item.title: Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: The Petrochemicals Shock Rippling Plastics (Podcast)】

item.title: HK’s Top Developer Gets $2.6 Billion Loan at Multi-Year Low Rate
item.description: Hong Kong’s largest developer Sun Hung Kai Properties Ltd. has secured a HK$20 billion ($2.6 billion) bank loan at its lowest borrowing cost in years, according to people familiar with the matter.
